title: Vestum gör miljardaffär – investmentbolag och Nokia i fokus
summary_md:
# Vestum gör miljardaffär – investmentbolag och Nokia i fokus

**Feed**: efn-borsfrukost  
**Published**: 2026-06-11  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The episode covers a cautious, somewhat puzzled market mood amid Middle East tensions, a hot US CPI print and rising rates, while equities hold up surprisingly well. Key Swedish news is Vestum's sale of its Flow Technologies arm to Nordic Capital (seen as a strong deal), a deep-dive on Nokia's Nvidia-driven rally (viewed as fundamentally expensive), and a review of investment companies favouring discounted laggards (Kinnevik, Latour) over premium-priced Öresund. Gold weakness and an EQT-to-Melker Schörling stake sale in Beijer Ref round out the discussion.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🔴 ORES — Öresund | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Stock has performed well but now trades at a ~20% premium to NAV, which hosts find unattractive.

#### Reasoning

Öresund was a top performer driven by strong holdings, but its re-rating to a ~20% premium leaves limited upside on a largely listed portfolio. Hosts explicitly say it is worth selling here, preferring to rotate into discounted peers.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the premium narrows back toward NAV
- if underlying holdings continue to outperform sharply

> *"trots att aktien har gått bra tycker vi faktiskt att den är värd att sälja i det här läget"*

---

### 🟢 KINV-B — Kinnevik | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

~40% discount (up to ~60% ex-cash), 97% unlisted, new leadership; a confidence discount that could close.

#### Reasoning

Kinnevik trades at a large confidence-driven discount with a sizable cash pile and an almost entirely unlisted portfolio that the market distrusts after writedowns. Hosts see upside if new management (Saxon, Stenbeck) can turn the ship and restore confidence in valuations.

#### Sell Triggers

- if further writedowns of unlisted holdings occur
- if new management fails to restore market confidence

> *"kan man bara få ordning där nu med Helena Saxon och Kristina Stenbäck och vrida om skutan här så tror jag det kan bli ganska spännande"*

---

### 🟢 LATO-B — Latour | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Normally trades at a premium but now without one; flagged as an interesting bottom-fishing buy.

#### Reasoning

Latour, a high-quality investment company that has historically commanded a premium, now trades without one, which the hosts view as an attractive entry. They group it with the discounted laggards they would buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if a premium re-emerges
- if underlying holdings deteriorate

> *"Vi ställer sig köp i de som har gått lite sämre. Där vi bottenfiskar i Kinnevik, Latour som alltid haft en premie egentligen. Som nu är utan premie då."*

---

### ⛔ NOKIA — Nokia | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Up 140% on the Nvidia deal but AI&Cloud is only ~8% of sales; ~18x EV/EBIT 2028 looks expensive on fundamentals.

#### Reasoning

Nokia's rally is largely a speculative AI option: the AI&Cloud segment grows fast but is a small share of revenue, with only ~6% modeled top-line growth. At ~18x forward EV/EBIT the hosts find it expensive unless margin expansion and growth materially exceed expectations beyond 2028.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI&Cloud growth stalls or stays a small revenue share
- if margins fail to expand beyond 2028
- further AI-sector derating like the Broadcom shock

> *"men jag tyckte väl det såg lite dyrt ut"*

---

### 🟡 VESTUM — Vestum | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 50%

Strong sale of Flow Technologies to Nordic Capital; fair value seen ~16-17 kr vs ~12.50 pre-news, but shares already opened +36%.

#### Reasoning

The divestment of the high-margin Flow Technologies (Watery) arm at ~20x earnings is a good deal, with hosts estimating fair value around 16-17 kr and a possible 1.5 kr distribution. However, the stock jumped ~31-36% on the news, leaving lower-quality 'niche products' segments behind and limited near-term upside after the pop.

#### Sell Triggers

- if remaining niche-product segments fail to grow
- if the sale proceeds are not returned to shareholders

> *"aktien borde ju ha gått upp till 16, 17 kronor någonstans"*

---

### 🟠 BEIJ-B — Beijer Ref | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

EQT sold its stake to Melker Schörling; a beneficiary of the shift to eco-friendly refrigerants; shares up ~10%.

#### Reasoning

Beijer Ref is positioned to win from the transition to environmentally friendly refrigerants, though execution has been bumpy. The ownership change to long-term holder Melker Schörling is constructive, and the hosts flag it as a name they have reason to examine more closely later.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the refrigerant transition continues to disappoint operationally
- if growth fails to reaccelerate

> *"det är ett intressant bolag som vi nog har facit att titta lite närmare på senare"*

---

### 🟠 CAMX — Camurus | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

FDA flagged a production-facility review (not the drug itself), delaying matters; shares down ~7%.

#### Reasoning

The FDA's request to review a manufacturing facility is unrelated to the drug's efficacy but introduces delay, which the hosts note is never positive. The ~7% drop reflects timing uncertainty rather than a fundamental product problem, warranting monitoring.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the facility issue escalates to affect approval
- if delays extend materially

> *"Det försenar saker och ting och det är aldrig positivt"*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **ORES** | Öresund | 🔴 SELL | MT | 70% | Stock has performed well but now trades at a ~20% premium to NAV, which hosts find unattractive. |
| **KINV-B** | Kinnevik | 🟢 BUY | LT | 60% | ~40% discount (up to ~60% ex-cash), 97% unlisted, new leadership; a confidence discount that could close. |
| **LATO-B** | Latour | 🟢 BUY | MT | 60% | Normally trades at a premium but now without one; flagged as an interesting bottom-fishing buy. |
| **NOKIA** | Nokia | ⛔ AVOID | LT | 60% | Up 140% on the Nvidia deal but AI&Cloud is only ~8% of sales; ~18x EV/EBIT 2028 looks expensive on fundamentals. |
| **VESTUM** | Vestum | 🟡 HOLD | ST | 50% | Strong sale of Flow Technologies to Nordic Capital; fair value seen ~16-17 kr vs ~12.50 pre-news, but shares already opened +36%. |
| **BEIJ-B** | Beijer Ref | 🟠 WATCH | MT | 50% | EQT sold its stake to Melker Schörling; a beneficiary of the shift to eco-friendly refrigerants; shares up ~10%. |
| **CAMX** | Camurus | 🟠 WATCH | ST | 40% | FDA flagged a production-facility review (not the drug itself), delaying matters; shares down ~7%. |
